{{short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|South Sudan women's national football team}}
{{Distinguish|Sudan national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=September 2015}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = South Sudan
| Nickname           = Bright Stars<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rnHYBBnyNGU|title=BBC Storyville 2014 Soccer Coach Zoran and his African Tigers|website=[[YouTube]]|access-date=5 September 2015|date=18 December 2014|archive-date=2 February 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160202045134/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rnHYBBnyNGU|url-status=live}}</ref>
| Badge             = 
| Association        = [[South Sudan Football Association]] (SSFA)
| Sub-confederation  = [[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations|CECAFA]] (East & Central Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Home Stadium       = [[Juba Stadium|Juba National Stadium]]
| Coach              = [[Nicolas Dupuis]]
| Captain            = [[Juma Genaro]]
| Most caps          = [[Peter Chol]] (46)
| Top scorer         = [[James Moga]]<br> [[Tito Okello (footballer)|Tito Okello]] (6)
| FIFA Trigramme     = SSD
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|SSD}}
| FIFA max           = 134
| FIFA max date      = November 2015
| FIFA min           = 205
| FIFA min date      = September 2013
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|South Sudan}}
| Elo max            = 171
| Elo max date       = 2016
| Elo min            = 188
| Elo min date       = November 2018
| pattern_la1        =_SSD2023H
| pattern_b1         =_SSD2023H
| pattern_ra1        =_SSD2023H
| pattern_sh1        =_SSD2023H
| pattern_so1        =
| leftarm1           = 
| body1              = 
| rightarm1          = 
| shorts1            = 
| socks1             = FFFFFF
| pattern_la2        = _ssd2122a
| pattern_b2         = _ssd2122a
| pattern_ra2        = _ssd2122a
| pattern_sh2        = _ssd2122a
| pattern_so2        =
| leftarm2           =
| body2              =
| rightarm2          =
| shorts2            =
| socks2             = 167934
| First game         = {{fb|SSD|2011}} 2–2 {{fb-rt|UGA}} <br /> ([[Juba]], [[South Sudan]]; 10 July 2012)
| Largest win        = {{fb|SSD|2011}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|DJI}} <br /> ([[Juba]], [[South Sudan]]; 28 March 2017)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|MOZ}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|SSD|2011}} <br /> ([[Maputo]], [[Mozambique]]; 18 May 2014) <br /> {{fb|SSD|2011}} 0–5 {{fb-rt|SEN}} <br /> ([[Juba]], [[South Sudan]]; 10 October 2025)
|Regional cup apps=4|Regional name=[[CECAFA Cup]]|Regional cup first=[[2012 CECAFA Cup|2012]]|Regional cup best=Quarter-finals ([[2015 CECAFA Cup|2015]])}}
The '''South Sudan national football team''' represents [[South Sudan]] in international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[South Sudan Football Association]], the governing body for football in [[South Sudan]].

==History==
[[Zoran Đorđević (football manager)|Zoran Đorđević]] was appointed on 25 May 2011 to oversee the national team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://africasacountry.com/2014/02/zoran-and-his-african-tigers|title=Zoran and his African Tigers|last=Jacobs|first=Sean|date=25 May 2011|work=Africas Country|access-date=15 January 2020|archive-date=16 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116000514/https://africasacountry.com/2014/02/zoran-and-his-african-tigers|url-status=live}}</ref> For their inaugural year the team was featured in a [[Storyville (TV series)|''Storyville'']] episode called ''Soccer Coach Zoran and his African Tigers''. The national team's first international fixture was due to be against the [[Kenya national football team|Kenya national team]] on 10 July 2011 as part of the country's independence celebrations.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.goss-online.org/magnoliaPublic/en/home/mainColumnParagraphs/00/content_files/file7/AgendaWord.doc|title=The Birth of the Republic of South Sudan|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://swap.stanford.edu/20110707121643/http://www.goss-online.org/magnoliaPublic/en/home/mainColumnParagraphs/00/content_files/file7/AgendaWord.doc|archive-date=July 7, 2011|format=DOC|access-date=December 22, 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.thejakartaglobe.com/basketball/worlds-newest-nation-set-to-step-into-sporting-arena/451201 |title=World's Newest Nation Set to Step Into Sporting Arena |author=Martell, Peter |work=Jakarta Globe |date=6 July 2011 |access-date=July 25, 2011 |archive-date=27 September 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120927093457/http://www.thejakartaglobe.com/basketball/worlds-newest-nation-set-to-step-into-sporting-arena/451201 |url-status=live }}</ref> However, in the event the opposition was provided by [[Tusker F.C.|Tusker]] of the [[Kenyan Premier League]],<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2011/jul/10/south-sudan-statehood-football-match |title=South Sudan marks statehood with football match |author=Rice, Xan |work=The Guardian |location=London |date=10 July 2011 |access-date=25 July 2011 |archive-date=30 September 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130930132402/http://www.theguardian.com/world/2011/jul/10/south-sudan-statehood-football-match |url-status=live }}</ref> alongside the first international fixture of the [[South Sudan men's national basketball team|national basketball team]]. The match was played at the [[Juba Stadium]]. South Sudan scored within ten minutes, but they later conceded three goals in a 3–1 defeat.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.kickoff.com/features/22897/independent-south-sudan-play-their-first-match-against-kenyas-tusker-fc.php |title=Independent South Sudan play first football match |work=Kickoff.com |date=11 July 2011 |access-date=24 December 2011 |archive-date=10 July 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150710160850/http://www.kickoff.com/features/22897/independent-south-sudan-play-their-first-match-against-kenyas-tusker-fc.php |url-status=dead }}</ref> South Sudan was officially admitted as a [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] member on 10 February 2012, at the 34th CAF Ordinary General Assembly hosted in [[Libreville]], [[Gabon]].<ref>[http://www.supersport.com/football/africa/news/120210/South_Sudan_admitted_as_a_member_of_Caf South Sudan admitted as a member of CAF] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120731152413/http://www.supersport.com/football/africa/news/120210/South_Sudan_admitted_as_a_member_of_Caf |date=31 July 2012 }}, SuperSport.com, Retrieved 10 February 2012.</ref> South Sudan was admitted as a [[FIFA]] member on 25 May 2012 at the second session of the 62nd FIFA Congress hosted in [[Budapest]], [[Hungary]].<ref>{{cite web|title=FIFA Congress fully backs reform process, appoints first woman to Executive; welcomes South Sudan as 209th FIFA member|url=https://www.fifa.com/aboutfifa/organisation/bodies/congress/news/newsid=1639773/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120527115747/http://www.fifa.com/aboutfifa/organisation/bodies/congress/news/newsid=1639773/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=27 May 2012|work=FIFA Congress|date=25 May 2012 |access-date=2 June 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.goal.com/en-india/news/143/africa/2012/05/27/3129200/south-sudan-become-fifas-209th-member |title=South Sudan become Fifa's 209th member |first=Kent |last=Mensah |publisher=Goal.com |date=27 May 2012 |access-date=24 June 2012 |archive-date=21 October 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131021101522/http://www.goal.com/en-india/news/143/africa/2012/05/27/3129200/south-sudan-become-fifas-209th-member |url-status=live }}</ref>

On 10 July 2012, South Sudan competed in its first full international match, a friendly against [[Uganda national football team|Uganda]] in [[Juba]].<ref name="July 2012">{{cite web|title=South Sudan enter FIFA rankings|url=https://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/news/south-sudan-enter-fifa-rankings-091317288--sow.html|publisher=Reuters|access-date=8 August 2012}}</ref> The match ended in a 2–2 draw, with [[James Moga]] and [[Richard Justin Lado]] scoring for South Sudan. This match resulted in South Sudan entering the FIFA rankings at the start of August in 199th place.<ref name="July 2012" />

The South Sudanese took part in their first ever international football tournament when they took part in the [[2012 CECAFA Cup]] in [[Uganda]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/18504126 South Sudan set for international debuts at Cecafa cups]  BBC. Retrieved 12 November 2012</ref><ref>[http://www.futaa.com/football/article/world-newest-state-set-for-cecafa-dabut World newest state set for CECAFA début] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130705114456/http://www.futaa.com/football/article/world-newest-state-set-for-cecafa-dabut |date=5 July 2013 }} Futaa.com. Retrieved 12 November 2012</ref> They were drawn in Group A alongside [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]], Kenya, and hosts Uganda.<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/20298092 Ethiopia get tough draw for 2012 Cecafa Challenge Cup]  BBC. Retrieved 12 November 2012</ref> The national team played their first match against Ethiopia, losing 1–0 through a [[Yonathan Kebede]] goal. In their next match, they lost 2–0 against Kenya. Their final match saw them suffer a 4–0 loss to Uganda.{{Cn|date=January 2023}}

South Sudan entered its first major international tournament in 2014, taking part in the [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]]. As one of the four lowest ranked national teams in Africa, it entered in the preliminary stage and was scheduled to play against [[Eritrea national football team|Eritrea]], who withdrew, thus qualifying South Sudan for the first qualifying round.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-soccer-africa-eritrea-idUKBREA2T0IH20140330|title=Eritrea withdraws from Nations Cup Qualification|publisher=Reuters.com|date=30 March 2014|access-date=5 July 2021|archive-date=2 April 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190402153817/https://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-soccer-africa-eritrea-idUKBREA2T0IH20140330|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cafonline.com/en-US/NewsCenter/News/NewsDetails?id=9gjB%2fpWFOwURPw4xsFjJ0w%3d%3d|title=Eritrea withdraws from Can 2015|publisher=Cafonline.com|date=30 March 2014|access-date=25 January 2015|archive-date=11 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150111175258/http://www.cafonline.com/en-US/NewsCenter/News/NewsDetails?id=9gjB%2fpWFOwURPw4xsFjJ0w%3d%3d|url-status=live}}</ref> There, they played [[Mozambique national football team|Mozambique]] over two legs, losing 5–0 at the [[Estádio do Zimpeto]] in [[Maputo]], but hosting a goalless draw in the second leg which was held at the [[Khartoum Stadium]] in Sudan due to the [[South Sudanese Civil War]].{{Cn|date=January 2023}}

On 5 September 2015, South Sudan achieved their first official victory, a 1–0 home win against Equatorial Guinea in [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations qualification Group C|2017 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]]. One month later South Sudan played their first ever match in FIFA World Cup Qualification, a 1–1 draw at home to [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]]. South Sudan would lose both return matches 4–0.{{Cn|date=January 2023}}

During [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2019 AFCON qualifying]] the Bright Stars achieved their record win, defeating [[Djibouti national football team|Djibouti]] 6–0 in Juba, however they lost all seven of their other matches meaning they remained among the lowest ranked teams in Africa.{{Cn|date=January 2023}}

In October 2019, in the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2021 AFCON preliminary round]], South Sudan won an away game for the first time, beating [[Seychelles national football team|Seychelles]] 1–0 in [[Victoria, Seychelles|Victoria]] to secure a 3–1 aggregate victory. This sees the Bright Stars advance to the qualifying group stage for the third consecutive edition.{{Cn|date=January 2023}}

South Sudan is invited by FIFA to take part in [[FIFA Arab Cup]] qualification.

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|South Sudan national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = Friendly
|date       = 28 May
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ERI}}
|score      = Cancelled 
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Asmara]], Eritrea
|stadium    = [[Cicero Stadium]]
|attendance =
|referee    =
|report     =
|result     = V
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 5 September
|time       = {{UTC|14:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 1–4
|team2      = {{fb|COD}}
|goals1     =
*[[Keer Majak|Majak]] {{goal|68}}
|goals2     =
*[[Cédric Bakambu|Bakambu]] {{goal|13||36}}
*[[Nathanaël Mbuku|Mbuku]] {{goal|45+1}}
*[[Yoane Wissa|Wissa]] {{goal|57}}
|stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
|location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
|attendance =
|referee    = Brighton Chimene ([[Zimbabwe Football Association|Zimbabwe]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020775
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 9 September
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MTN}}
|score      = 0–0
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Nouadhibou Municipal Stadium]]
|location   = [[Nouadhibou]], Mauritania
|attendance =
|referee    = Mehrez Malki ([[Tunisian Football Federation|Tunisia]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020777
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 10 October
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 0–5
|team2      = {{fb|SEN}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Ismaïla Sarr|I. Sarr]] {{goal|29||54}}
*[[Sadio Mané|Mané]] {{goal|46}}
*[[Nicolas Jackson|Jackson]] {{goal|60|pen.}}
*[[Cherif Ndiaye|Ndiaye]] {{goal|75}}
|stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
|location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
|attendance =
|referee    = Abdou Abdel Mefire ([[Cameroonian Football Federation|Cameroon]]) 
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021040
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 13 October
|time       = {{UTZ|14:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 0–0
|team2      = {{fb|TOG}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
|location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
|attendance =
|referee    = Lamin Jammeh ([[Gambia Football Federation|Gambia]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021041
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup qualification]]
|date       = 25 November
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|3}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SYR}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10013/290556/291903/400020254
|goals1     = 
*[[Mohammad Al-Hallaq|Al-Hallaq]] {{goal|52}}
*[[Mahmoud Al Mawas|Al Mawas]] {{goal|59}}
|goals2     = 
|location   = [[Doha]], Qatar
|stadium    = [[Grand Hamad Stadium]]
|attendance = 7,294
|referee    = [[Amin Omar]] ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|result     = L
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON Qualifier]]
|date       = 26 March
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|DJI}}
|score      = 0–4
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|goals1     = 
|goals2     = 
*[[Teng Kuol|Kuol]] {{goal|31}}
*[[Justin Bangasi|Bangasi]] {{goal|49}}
*[[Peter Maker|Maker]] {{goal|66}}
*[[Keer Majak|Majak]] {{goal|81}}
|stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
|location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
|attendance = 0 ([[Behind closed doors (sport)|Behind closed doors]])<ref>{{cite web |last= |first= |date=20 March 2026|title=South Sudan to host Djibouti in AFCON qualifiers behind closed doors|url=https://www.sudanspost.com/south-sudan-to-host-djibouti-in-afcon-qualifiers-behind-closed-doors/|website=sudanspost.com|location=[[Juba]]|publisher=[[Sudans Post]]|access-date=22 March 2026}}</ref>
|referee    = [[Tewodros Mitiku]] ([[Ethiopian Football Federation|Ethiopia]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/news/south-sudan-cruise-past-djibouti-4-0-in-totalenergies-caf-afcon-2027-qualifying-opener/
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON Qualifier]]
|date       = 29 March
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|DJI}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Samuel Akinbinu|Akinbinu]] {{Goal|77}}
|stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
|location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
|attendance = 0 ([[Behind closed doors (sport)|Behind closed doors]])
|referee    = Retṧelisitsoe Molise ([[Lesotho Football Association|Lesotho]])
|report     = [https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-29/south-sudan-vs-djibouti/4048692 Report]
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|ANG}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, South Sudan
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ANG}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Angola
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|MWI}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, South Sudan
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MWI}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Malawi
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|EGY}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SSD}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Egypt
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|EGY}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, South Sudan
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}

==Coaches==
[[File:Stefano-wiki.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Stefano Cusin]] became the manager of the South Sudan national football team in 2011]]
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.

{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Stephen Constantine]] (2009–2011)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Malesh Soro (2011–2012)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Ismail Balanga (2012)
*{{flagicon|SRB}} [[Zoran Đorđević (football manager)|Zoran Đorđević]] (2012–2013)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Ismail Balanga (2013–2014)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} [[Salyi Lolaku Samuel]] (2014)
*{{flagicon|KOR}} [[Lee Sung-jea]] (2014–2015)
*{{flagicon|UGA}} [[Leo Adraa]] (2015–2016)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Joseph Malesh (2016)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Elya Wako (2017)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Bilal Felix Komoyangi (2017–2018)
*{{flagicon|ALG}} [[Ahcene Aït-Abdelmalek]] (2018)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Ramsey Sebit (2018)
*{{flagicon|CMR}} [[Cyprian Besong Ashu]] (2019–2021)
*{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Stefano Cusin]] (2021–2023)
*{{flagicon|SSD|2011}} Deng Aleer (2023)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Nicolas Dupuis]] (2023–2026)
*{{flagicon|CMR}} [[Cyprian Besong Ashu]] (2026-)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B]] matches against [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] and [[Sudan national football team|Sudan]] on 21 and 25 March 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Coach Nicola Dupuis released his travelling squad for the FIFA 2026 World Cup Qualifiers to face DR. Congo and Sudan |url=https://x.com/ssfa_com/status/1901600469312504291/photo/1 |website=X |publisher=South Sudan Football Association |date=17 March 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 25 March 2025, after the match against [[Sudan national football team|Sudan]].''

{{Nat fs g start}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Majak Mawith]]|age={{Bda|1999|9|18|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Godwill Yugusuk]]|age={{bda|1999|11|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Kator FC|Kator]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Rashid Toha]]|age={{Bda|1997|10|9|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=1|club=[[Kenya Police FC|Kenya Police]]|clubnat=KEN}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Athir Thomas]]|age={{Bda|1987|2|14|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=1|club=[[Al Merreikh FC (South Sudan)|Al Merreikh]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Samuel Taban]]|age={{bda|2002|10|29|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[David Omot Sebit]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|10|28|df=yes}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Kator FC|Kator]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Paul Puk Pal]]|age={{bda|2000|2|12|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Al Merreikh FC (South Sudan)|Al Merreikh]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Rashid Okocha]]|age={{Bda|1993|12|10|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[National Enterprises Corporation FC|NEC]]|clubnat=UGA}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Bichiok]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|08|25|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Nairobi United FC|Nairobi United]]|clubnat=KEN}}
{{Nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ivan Wani]]|age={{Bda|1998|12|12|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Joseph Malish Manase]]|age={{Bda|2002|7|27|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[William Gama]]|age={{bda|2002|12|14|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=1|club=[[Al-Malakia FC|Al-Malakia]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Peter Chan (footballer)|Peter Chan]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|11|28|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Olympic FC (South Sudan)|Olympic]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Pap Chol]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|11|30|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Kator FC|Kator]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mario Albano]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|04|23|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nelson Victor Elia]]|age={{bda|2002|9|30|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Al Merreikh FC (South Sudan)|Al Merreikh]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nhiak Agany]]|age{{birth date and age|2000|03|29|df=yes}}=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Holy Family FC|Holy Family]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Simon Kuoto'o]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|03|29|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tito Okello (footballer)|Tito Okello]]|age={{Bda|1996|1|7|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=6|club=[[Abi al Ashar]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Paul Jawa]]|age={{Bda|2004|7|15|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[FC Fleury 91 (men)|Fleury 91]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Yohanna Paulino]]|age={{Bda|1999|10|6|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Lazarus Laku]]|age={{birth date and age|2008|05|29|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[IFK Göteborg]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Angufi Mudasiri]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|11|28|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Koryom FC|Koryom]]|clubnat=SSD}}
{{Nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
{{nat fs r start}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Juma Genaro]]|age={{Bda|1986|2|28|df=y}}|caps=34|goals=0|club=[[Al-Merrikh SC|Al-Merrikh]]|clubnat=SUD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Dario Konyang]]|age={{bda|2004|6|26|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Madut Nyuol]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|01|29|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Nicolas Madeng]]|age={{Bda|1998|1|7|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Atendele Geriga]]|age={{Bda|1995|5|5|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[Arua Hill Sports Club|Arua Hill]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Rehan Angier]]|age={{Bda|2002|1|1|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Munuki FC|Munuki]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Benjamin Laku Lotelek|Benjamin Laku]]|age={{Bda|2006|6|27|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Emmanuel Maku]]|age={{Bda|2006|10|20|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[David Djamas]]|age={{Bda|2004|4|21|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Achyronas-Onisilos|Achyronas]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Oman Mobil]]|age={{Bda|2003|12|2|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Al-Malakia FC|Al-Malakia]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Loki Emmanuel]]|age={{Bda|2001|11|14|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=1|club=[[Bright Stars FC|Bright Stars]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Peter Maker]]|age={{Bda|1994|1|1|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=0|club=[[FK Radnički Sremska Mitrovica|Radnički S. Mitrovica]]|clubnat=SRB|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Peter Chol]]|age={{Bda|1994|10|23|df=y}}|caps=41|goals=4|club=[[Kator FC|Kator]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Gaddafi Wahab]]|age={{Bda|1995|12|11|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[National Enterprises Corporation FC|NEC]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mandela Malish]]|age={{Bda|1999|10|10|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Atlabara FC|Atlabara]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Omar Luate]]|age={{bda|2001|10|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Charles Ouma]]|age={{Bda|1999|6|2|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Administration Police F.C.|Kenya Police]]|clubnat=KEN|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Joseph Dhata]]|age={{bda|2002|9|5|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Express FC|Express]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nevello Yoseke]]|age={{bda|1996|3|17|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Monterey Bay FC|Monterey Bay]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jackson Morgan]]|age={{Bda|1998|8|18|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Bentleigh Greens SC|Bentleigh Greens]] |clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|SUD}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Manyumow Achol]]|age={{Bda|2000|12|10|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[PFC Septemvri Sofia|Septemvri Sofia]]|clubnat=BUL|latest=v. {{fb|TOG}}; 5 June 2024<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs break}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Valentino Yuel]]|age={{Bda|1994|10|12|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=3|club=[[Eastern District SA|Eastern District]]|clubnat=HKG|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Data Elly]]|age={{Bda|1999|5|2|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Onduparaka FC|Onduparaka]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ebon Malish]]|age={{Bda|2004|3|23|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=2|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ronald Innocent]]|age={{Bda|2002|3|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Lucera Calcio]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Emmanuel Jowang]]|age={{Bda|2002|8|29|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Al-Malakia FC|Al-Malakia]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tito Lukciir]]|age={{Bda|2004|10|22|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Simba FC|Simba]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tot Maet]]|age=''Unknown''|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Unknown''|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ayom Majok]]|age={{Bda|2003|1|1|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Marsaxlokk F.C.|Marsaxlokk]]|clubnat=MLT|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Patrick Oleyo]]|age={{Bda|2005|8|5|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Viva Star FC|Viva Star]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 19 November 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[David Majak Chan]]|age={{Bda|2000|10|10|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Tusker F.C.|Tusker]]|clubnat=KEN|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Alfred Leku]]|age={{Bda|1997|9|16|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Administration Police F.C.|Kenya Police]]|clubnat=KEN|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ajak Riak]]|age={{bda|2000|12|12|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Adelaide United FC|Adelaide United]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Madit Mayor]]|age={{Bda|2001|2|2|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Atlabara FC|Atlabara]]|clubnat=SSD|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Francis Onekalit]]|age={{Bda|1996|8|18|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[SC Villa|Villa]]|clubnat=UGA|latest=v. {{fb|UGA}}; 15 October 2024}}

{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Machop Chol]]|age={{Bda|1998|11|14|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Ansan Greeners FC|Ansan Greeners]]|clubnat=KOR|latest=v. {{fb|SUD}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|10 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=South Sudan |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/283/South_Sudan.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=19 June 2022 |archive-date=19 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230219071539/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/283/2023/South_Sudan.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with South Sudan.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!style="width:30px|Rank
!style="width:150px;"|Player
!style="width:50px|Caps
!style="width:50px|Goals
!style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Peter Chol]]'''
|46
|4
|2015–present
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Juma Genaro]]'''
|38
|0
|2012–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Peter Maker]]'''
|33
|0
|2018–present
|-
|rowspan=3|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dominic Abui Pretino]]
|30
|4
|2013–2021
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Athir Thomas]]'''{{efn|group=players|Athir Thomas also appeared in 4 matches for Sudan}}
|30
|1
|2012–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Ivan Wani]]'''
|30
|0
|2020–present
|-
|rowspan=2|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Leon Uso Khamis]]
|29
|3
|2012–2022
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Tito Okello (footballer)|Tito Okello]]'''
|29
|6
|2020–present
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Majak Mawith]]'''
|25
|0
|2019–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Rashid Toha]]'''
|25
|1
|2021–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Jamesmoga.JPG|200px|right|thumb|[[James Moga]] is South Sudan's joint top scorer with 6 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!style="width:30px|Rank
!style="width:150px;"|Player
!style="width:50px|Goals
!style="width:50px|Caps
!style="width:50px|Ratio
!style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|rowspan=2|1
|style="text-align: left;"|[[James Moga]]{{efn|group=players|name=Moga|James Moga also appeared in 26 matches for Sudan, scoring 6 goals}}
|6
|18
|{{#expr:6/18 round 2}}
|2012–2017
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Tito Okello (footballer)|Tito Okello]]'''
|6
|29
|{{#expr:6/29 round 2}}
|2020–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Ebon Ezibon]]'''
|5
|6
|{{#expr:5/6 round 2}}
|2024–present
|-
|rowspan=3|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Joseph Kuch Nyuar]]
|4
|8
|{{#expr:4/8 round 2}}
|2017–2019
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dominic Abui Pretino]]
|4
|30
|{{#expr:4/30 round 2}}
|2013–2021
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Peter Chol]]'''
|4
|46
|{{#expr:4/46 round 2}}
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan=4|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Sebit Bruno]]
|3
|10
|{{#expr:3/10 round 2}}
|2015–2016
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Atak Lual]]
|3
|13
|{{#expr:3/13 round 2}}
|2014–2018
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Valentino Yuel]]'''
|3
|24
|{{#expr:3/24 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Leon Uso Khamis]]
|3
|29
|{{#expr:3/29 round 2}}
|2012–2022
|-
|}

'''Notes:'''
{{notelist|group=players}}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
|[[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]] to [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|0
|5
|5
|3
|19
|- 
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
! 
!{{Tooltip|0/2|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
! 
! 
! 
! 
! 
!
!14
!0
!7
!7
!5
!26
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
! rowspan="39" width="1" |
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=26|''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|colspan=8 rowspan=26|''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 Africa Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Cup of Nations|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]] 
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|6
|1
|0
|5
|3
|15
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|8
|1
|0
|7
|8
|20
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|8
|3
|0
|5
|5
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|8
|3
|0
|5
|10
|15
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|8
|1
|2
|5
|7
|13
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
| colspan="6" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/35||0||0||0||0||0||0
!40
!9
!3
!28
!33
!75
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
|-
!Total||–||0/8||–||–||–||–||–||–
|}

===CECAFA Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[CECAFA Cup]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 4
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1973 CECAFA Cup|1973]] to {{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2010 CECAFA Cup|2010]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2011 CECAFA Cup|2011]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2012 CECAFA Cup|2012]]||Group stage||11th||3||0||0||3||0||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2013 CECAFA Cup|2013]]||Group stage||10th||3||0||0||3||2||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia}} [[2015 CECAFA Cup|2015]]||Quarter-finals||6th||4||2||2||0||4||0
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2017 CECAFA Cup|2017]]||Group stage||9th||3||0||1||2||1||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2019 CECAFA Cup|2019]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
!Total||Quarter-finals||4/40||13||2||3||8||7||22
|}

===FIFA Arab Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA Arab Cup]] record
! rowspan="40" width="1%" |
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[1963 Arab Nations Cup|1963]] to ''[[2009 Arab Nations Cup|2009]]''{{Ref|n1|1}}||colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''||colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|Sudan}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2012 Arab Nations Cup|2012]]||colspan=8|''Not invited''||colspan=8|''Not invited''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2021 FIFA Arab Cup|2021]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|0{{NoteTag|The qualification match against Jordan was awarded to her due to a forfeit from South Sudan as multiple players and staff had positive COVID-19 test results upon their arrival in the qualifications hosts Qatar.}}||0||0||1||0||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup|2025]]
| colspan="8" |''Did not qualify''
|[[2025 FIFA Arab Cup qualification|1]] ||0||0||1||0||2
|-
!Total|| ||0/3||0||0||0||0||0||0
!1||0||0||2||0||5
|}
{{note|n1|1}} <small>The [[2009 Arab Nations Cup|2009]] edition was cancelled during qualification.</small>

==Head-to-head record==
{{#lst:South Sudan national football team results|Record by opponent}}

==Dual-internationals==
The following South Sudanese international footballers have also played for [[Sudan national football team]] before the country's independence:
*[[James Moga]]&nbsp;– forward for Sudan. Played for them in [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification]] and [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification]]. Played 14 matches and scored six goals.
*[[Richard Justin Lado]]&nbsp;– Experienced defender in club football for Khartoum 3 and played for Sudan between 2008 and 2012.
*[[Athir Thomas]]&nbsp;– defender in Sudan before the country's partition.
*[[Roy Gulwak]]&nbsp;– Goalkeeper who represented Sudan in two [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]] matches in 2009, conceding two goals.
*[[Khamis Martin]]&nbsp;– Played one international game for Sudan in 2010.

==Notes & references==
===Notes===
{{reflist|group=nb}}
{{reflist|group=note}}
{{notelist}}

===References===
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Portal|Association football}}
{{commons category}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/ssd/ South Sudan] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/SSD South Sudan] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.rsssf.org/tablesz/zsoed-intres.html South Sudan&nbsp;– List of International Matches] {{in lang|en}}

{{South Sudan national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|bg = white
|fg = black
|bordercolor=#E22028
|list1={{South Sudan national football team managers}}
}}
{{Football in South Sudan}}
{{CECAFA Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of South Sudan}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:South Sudan National Football Team}}
[[Category:South Sudan national football team| ]]
[[Category:Football in South Sudan|National]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:2011 establishments in South Sudan]]
